Archive / Issue #45

Winter's Last Dance

Wednesday, March 4, 2026

Editor's Note

"Every year I think I'm over winter and then the last skating weekend on the Canal happens and I'm not. There's something genuinely melancholy and beautiful about it — the ice rougher than it was in January, everyone out there knowing it's the last time. Go skate."
Featured This Week

Last Weekend on the Rideau Canal Skateway

Saturday & Sunday · Until the ice closesRideau Canal Skateway – Full length, 7.8km

The world's largest naturally frozen skating rink is in its final days of the season. Skate the full 7.8km from Dow's Lake to the NAC, grab a Beavertail at one of the hut stops, and take it in — it won't be back until January. Free to skate, rentals available at major access points.

Get Tickets & Details

Getting this from a friend?

Subscribe free and get Ottawa's best in your inbox every Thursday.

Free, every Thursday. Unsubscribe anytime.

This Week's Highlights

Winterlude – Crystal Garden Ice Sculptures

Friday–Sunday · 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM (final weekend)Confederation Park, Laurier Ave W

The renowned ice sculpture competition ends its run this weekend with 30 international teams' completed works on display. The Chinese team's five-metre dragon is drawing serious crowds. Free, dress warm.

More info

Duo Concertante at Carleton Dominion-Chalmers Centre

Saturday, March 7 · 7:30 PMDominion-Chalmers United Church, 355 Cooper St

Ottawa's beloved classical duo — violin and piano — perform a program of Schubert, Prokofiev, and a world premiere from a local composer. Intimate venue, extraordinary acoustics, tickets from $25.

More info

Textile & Thread Exhibition at Studio 22

Opens March 6, runs through April 30Studio 22 Gallery, 22 Elgin St

A stunning new show from six Ottawa-area textile artists working in weaving, embroidery, and mixed media. The standout piece is a 12-metre woven installation that fills the gallery's main room. Free entry.

More info

Sens vs Leafs – Home Game

Saturday, March 7 · 7:00 PMCanadian Tire Centre, 1000 Palladium Dr, Kanata

The Ottawa–Toronto rivalry brings the city's hockey energy to its peak. There's electricity in the Tire Centre on nights like this. Tickets still available in the upper bowl.

More info

Family Corner

Winterlude – Snow Slides at Jacques-Cartier Park

All weekend · 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM·Jacques-Cartier Park, Gatineau (5 min from Parliament)

Giant snow slides, ice mazes, and the world of ice sculptures — all free for families. Cross the Portage Bridge and spend a morning exploring the Gatineau side of Winterlude.

More info

Rideau Centre Family Skate

Sunday, March 8 · 1:00 PM – 3:30 PM·Marion Dewar Plaza (outdoors, beside Rideau Centre)

Free outdoor skating on the downtown rink with music and hot chocolate from the nearby stands. Rentals available on-site. The last organized family skate of the season.

More info

Ottawa Public Library – Bilingual Winter Story Time

Saturday, March 7 · 10:00 AM·Beaverbrook Branch, 2500 Campeau Dr, Kanata

A bilingual (English/French) winter-themed story time for ages 2–6, with songs, puppets, and a simple craft to take home. Free, drop-in welcome.

More info
